title: penstemon eatonii subsp. exsertus
intro: Penstemon eatonii subsp. exsertus, commonly known as the exserted beardtongue, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is native to the southwestern United States and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Herbal preparations of Penstemon eatonii subsp. exsertus can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and have been used to promote wound healing, alleviate coughs, and soothe sore throats.
